description: Update bengali-cyberbullying-transformer.ipynb (Track 2): enhance markdown cells, add literature comparison with v6 results showing parameter efficiency, add meaningful visualizations (currently has minimal viz - only training curves), and update documentation to clearly position this as the secondary track.
steps: Read the full transformer notebook structure from bengali-cyberbullying-transformer.ipynb

Update Cell 0 (title markdown): Rewrite to position as Track 2. Add comparison summary showing that while this model (110M params) achieves F1=0.8428, the lightweight hierarchical v6 (3.3M params) achieves F1=0.8551. Explain the trade-off: transformer has better ROC-AUC (0.9617 vs 0.9526) but worse F1 due to threshold sensitivity, and is 33x larger.
Add a new markdown cell after Section 4 (after the split): Add a brief EDA section with visualization showing class distribution in the split data.

Update Cell 14 (Section 14 notes markdown): Expand notes to include detailed analysis of why the transformer underperforms the hierarchical model despite having 33x more parameters. Key reasons: (1) the hierarchical two-stage approach provides better gradient flow for minority classes, (2) stage2-toxic-only training eliminates neutral noise, (3) the dataset (12.7k samples) is relatively small for fine-tuning 110M params, (4) the reconstruction formula P(class)=P(toxic)*P(class|toxic) provides built-in calibration.

Add a new code cell before the final markdown (before Cell 14): Add a comprehensive visualization section including: (a) per-class precision/recall/F1 bar chart, (b) confusion matrices for all 4 classes, (c) ROC and PR curves, (d) comparison bar chart showing v6 vs transformer per-class F1 side by side.

Add a literature comparison code cell: Create a comparison table similar to v6 but including both our models. Show parameter count, F1, ROC-AUC, CPU-deployable flag. Add same literature references as v6 notebook for consistency.

Add a conclusion markdown cell: Summarize that while Track 2 validates the approach on a pretrained model, the hierarchical lightweight model (Track 1 primary / v6) is preferred due to: 33x smaller, better F1, CPU-deployable, and faster inference.
Validate the entire notebook JSON is valid and all code cells parse with ast.parse()
acceptance_criteria: All markdown cells contain clear analysis of the transformer approach and its comparison to v6
At least 3 new meaningful visualizations are added (per-class metrics bar chart, confusion matrices, comparison chart)
A literature comparison table is present with the same references as v6 notebook plus both internal models
The notebook clearly states that v6 hierarchical (3.3M) outperforms this transformer (110M) at F1
All code cells parse with ast.parse() without errors
verification: python3 -c "import json, ast; nb=json.load(open('bengali-cyberbullying-transformer.ipynb')); [ast.parse(''.join(c['source'])) for c in nb['cells'] if c['cell_type']=='code']; print('All cells parse OK')"
